title: How Much Does It Cost to Start a Restaurant in New Hampshire?
content:
Starting a Restaurant in New Hampshire typically costs between $184,500 and $791,000. That is about 5% higher than the national average of $175,000-$750,000, driven primarily by New Hampshire's above-average cost of living, labor rates, and commercial rents.

New Hampshire has no state income tax and no sales tax, making it one of the most tax-friendly states for small businesses. Proximity to Boston creates access to a large metro market while maintaining lower costs. The state does levy a Business Profits Tax on net income, but the lack of sales tax is a significant advantage for retail and food businesses.

From a regulatory standpoint, New Hampshire has specific requirements to plan for. New Hampshire has no state income tax, which means more of your business profits stay in your pocket compared to the national average. New Hampshire follows the federal minimum wage of $7.25/hour, though market rates for skilled workers are typically much higher.

Cost Breakdown for New Hampshire

Costs adjusted for New Hampshire's cost of living, labor rates, and commercial rents.

New Hampshire Business Requirements

To legally operate a restaurant in New Hampshire, you will need to:

  • Form an LLC or business entity - The filing fee in New Hampshire is $100, with a $100 annual report fee.
  • Obtain a business license - Requirements and fees vary by city. Contact your local Manchester or Nashua clerk's office for specifics.
  • No state sales tax registration needed - New Hampshire does not levy a state sales tax, simplifying your compliance.
  • No state income tax - New Hampshire does not levy a state income tax on business profits, which is a meaningful advantage for profitability.
  • Get business insurance - General liability insurance is essential in New Hampshire. Most landlords and clients require at least $1 million in coverage.

Why New Hampshire Costs Differ

New Hampshire's cost of living is 8% above the national average, which affects everything from supplies to services you need to purchase. Labor costs in New Hampshire are near the national average. Commercial rents in New Hampshire are close to the national average, with significant variation between Manchester and smaller cities.

New Hampshire-Specific Tips

  • Commercial rents in New Hampshire are below the national average, which means you can get more square footage for your money. Use that to your advantage with a layout that maximizes seating and kitchen efficiency.

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does it cost to start a restaurant in New Hampshire?

Starting a restaurant in New Hampshire typically costs between $184,500 and $791,000, depending on your location within the state, your business model, and how lean you launch. Costs in Manchester tend to run higher than in smaller New Hampshire cities like Portsmouth.

Do I need a special license to operate a restaurant in New Hampshire?

Yes. At minimum, you need a New Hampshire business license and any industry-specific permits required by your city or county. LLC formation costs $100 in New Hampshire, plus a $100 annual report fee. Check with your local Manchester or Nashua city hall for specific requirements.

Does New Hampshire have a state income tax?

No. New Hampshire has no state income tax, which is a significant advantage for business owners. Your business profits are only subject to federal income tax and self-employment tax, which means more of your revenue stays in your business compared to states with income taxes of 5-10%.

Is New Hampshire a good state to start a restaurant?

New Hampshire has above-average costs but also a large consumer market. New Hampshire has no state income tax and no sales tax, making it one of the most tax-friendly states for small businesses.
